title: Recursive Containers, Stack Parsing, Depth, and Recovery · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for recursive containers, stack parsing, depth, and recovery and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, parser, template, validator, host, CDN, DNS, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded nested-block parser and recovery trace. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Define base case, recursive reduction, returned state, source advancement, and invariant for nested document structures.
Use an explicit open-container stack where depth, continuation, closure, and source ownership need controlled iteration.
Reject or recover from excessive nesting before Python recursion, memory, or diagnostic output becomes unbounded.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Recursion Contract has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Recursion Contract, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Recursion Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Recursion Contract, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz publishing team handles case 1236 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound recursive containers, guarantee source advancement, and retain recovery evidence; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define base case, recursive reduction, returned state, source advancement, and invariant for nested document structures.

For Static-site publishing and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Recursion Contract, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Recursive code is correct when it eventually stops on familiar fixtures.

options.text: Declare Static-site publishing and Python reader outcome, content authority, revision, source identity, parser scope, trust policy, route base, accessibility need, and byte, depth, work, and output budgets for the quiz Recursion Contract case

Predict Static-site publishing and Python admission, decoding, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery outcomes for the quiz Recursion Contract case
Run the smallest deterministic Static-site publishing and Python publication model and retain exact intermediate and changed-case results for the quiz Recursion Contract case

Change one Static-site publishing and Python document, delimiter, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, deployment, or recovery condition, verify bounded rejection or repair, and state production transfer limits for the quiz Recursion Contract case

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Nesting Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Nesting Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz publishing team handles case 4508 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound recursive containers, guarantee source advancement, and retain recovery evidence; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test mixed quote, list, code, paragraph, blank-line, and malformed nesting against tree and source-span invariants.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Nesting Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Nesting Evidence through the concrete bounded nested-block parser and recovery trace case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Nesting Evidence, keep Nesting Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test mixed quote, list, code, paragraph, blank-line, and malformed nesting against tree and source-span invariants.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test mixed quote, list, code, paragraph, blank-line, and malformed nesting against tree and source-span invariants.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Nesting Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: One deeply nested happy path covers container interactions.
